events.title: Lifeguard in Training (LIT) Prep Clinic
events.description: Session Dates: May 5-28 and June 2-25 $165 per session Instructors Name: Mike and Maggie The LIT/Lifeguard Prep Class is designed to prepare youth and teens for future participation in the Lifeguard in Training program and eventual lifeguard certification. This class focuses on developing swim endurance, physical readiness, water confidence, and foundational rescue knowledge in a structured, supportive environment. This is a preparatory, non-certifying program intended to build skills and confidence before formal LIT or lifeguard certification courses. Register at front desk or online at stfrancisparishlbi.org

location.address: 4700 Long Beach Blvd, Long Beach, New Jersey, 08008

events.title: The Spring Derby is Here – Beloved Annual Surf Fishing Tournament Has Kicked Off
events.description: The Spring Derby is here – this beloved annual surf fishing tournament kicked off last weekend, and will run until 6 p.m. on June 21. For three months, anglers can target striped bass and bluefish from the beaches of Long Beach Island to win cash prizes. Fish must be caught by means of rod and reel in accordance with New Jersey state and federal regulations, and fish must be caught from the shores of LBI only – the ocean, inlet, bay and jetties. No stripers or blues caught by boat are accepted for this tournament. Registration is just $20 and is available online, at lbisfc.com/sign-up-weigh-in , or at one of the three participating tackle shops: Fisherman’s Headquarters in Ship Bottom, Jingles Bait and Tackle in Beach Haven and Surf City Bait and Tackle. These shops also serve as the weigh-in stations for the contest. For more information, including all tournament rules and requirements, visit lbisfc.com/lbi-spring-surf-fishing-tournament . Check out the three tackle shops’ Facebook pages as well for updates, or give them a call at 609-494-5739 (Fisherman’s Headquarters), 609-492-2795 (Jingles) or 609-494-2333 (Surf City).

events.description: The Barnegat Democratic Club sponsors a presentation by the Jersey Shore Immigrant Support Coalition. Club President Marianne Clemente said the coalition is a volunteer-driven, mutual-aid collective established to support immigrant communities along the New Jersey coast. Light refreshments will be served. For more information, call Clemente at 212-249-6292.

events.title: Southern Regional Offers Musical Experiences
events.description: For those seeking an enjoyable, musical experience during the coming weeks, the school district’s performing arts department will present its annual spring concerts inside the Joseph P. Echle Performing Arts Center. On Thursday, May 14 , the district strings program will delight the audience. A high school band concert is slated for Friday, May 15 , and the middle school band and chorus will follow up with some sweet sounds in a concert set for Tuesday, May 19 . The annual Southern Regional Dance and Theatre Showcase closes out the performance schedule on Wednesday, May 27 . All performances begin at 7 p.m., and tickets are $2 per person. All proceeds benefit the SRHS music department scholarship fund. Tickets can be purchased in advance at srtickets.net

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘A Grand Night For Singing’
events.description: A Grand Night For Singing Taste and imagination, the two key ingredients for a first-rate revue, abound in this fresh take on the Rodgers & Hammerstein canon. This new R&H musical opened the 1994 Broadway season with flair and distinction, garnering wildly enthusiastic notices and earning two Tony nominations, including Best Musical. With innovative musical arrangements, including “I’m Gonna Wash That Man Right Outa My Hair,” “Shall We Dance,” and “How Do You Solve A Problem Like Maria,” proving how terrifically up-to-date the remarkable songs of R&H remain. May 24, 28, 31, June 2, 4, 7 at 2:00 p.m. May 23, 24, 29, 30, June 4, 5, 6 at 8:00 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Long Beach Island Historical Museum Hosts 50th Anniversary Open House
events.description: Our Story. Your Museum. Celebrating 50 years Join us for a milestone celebration as the museum marks 50 years of preserving Long Beach Island’s history — made possible entirely by the dedication of our volunteers, past and present. Explore the museum through guided and self-guided tours, connect with the community and learn some LBI history that maybe you didn't know. Be sure to register for the guided tours. We will end the celebration with a special tribute to John Coyle, whose vision helped bring the museum to life. Register and learn more at lbihistoricalmuseum.org

location.address: 129 Engleside Ave, Beach Haven, New Jersey, 08008

events.title: Free Throw Competition Returns to Long Beach Township
events.description: Lace up: The free throw competition returns to Long Beach Township on May 30. Sign up now to take part in the second annual contest, to be held on the basketball court outside the town hall in Brant Beach. The tourney will run from 4 to 6 p.m. The event will consist of four age groups: 7-8, 9-11, 12-17 and 18-plus. In the first round, ages 7-8 and 9-11 will take 10 shots, ages 12-17 will take 15 shots and ages 18-plus will take 20 shots. The two players who make the most shots in each age group will move on to the final round. Tie breakers are sudden death. The final round is comprised of the same number of shots for each age group, with one competitor per group crowned the winner. First-place prizes for each age group are as follows: for group 7-8, a prize packet worth $100 from Bricks & Minfigs, Fantasy Island and Shore Good Donuts; for group 9-11, a prize packet worth $100 from Bricks & Minfigs, Shore Good Donuts and Thundering Surf; and, for group 12-17 as well as for group 18-plus, prize packets worth $100 from Jersey Girl Kitchen and Fratello’s Market. Basketballs will be provided for the event. For more information and to register, visit longbeachtownship.com/2nd-annual-free-throw-basketball-competition

events.title: Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

location.address: 614 E Bay Ave, Barnegat Township, New Jersey, 08005

events.title: Emily Provance Holds Presentation at Barnegat Friends Meeting
events.description: Emily Provance visit and discussion of communities and violence in an increasingly fractured society Emily Provance, “Quaker Emily”, will join Barnegat Friends in person on Sunday, May 31 at 2 pm. Her presentation will be shared via Zoom. Emily will discuss communities and political violence. A recent FIRE survey found that 34% of college students now say it is sometimes acceptable to respond to objectionable speech with violence. That’s a scary trend regardless of where we stand politically. Immediately prior to the visit, Emily will be in Northern Ireland, where she will be looking at the history of the Troubles and how that is parallel (and not) to current conditions in the United States, then pulling from Quaker experience and Quaker theology to talk about how we respond to an increasingly fractured society.

events.title: The Spring Derby is Here – Beloved Annual Surf Fishing Tournament Has Kicked Off
events.description: The Spring Derby is here – this beloved annual surf fishing tournament kicked off last weekend, and will run until 6 p.m. on June 21. For three months, anglers can target striped bass and bluefish from the beaches of Long Beach Island to win cash prizes. Fish must be caught by means of rod and reel in accordance with New Jersey state and federal regulations, and fish must be caught from the shores of LBI only – the ocean, inlet, bay and jetties. No stripers or blues caught by boat are accepted for this tournament. Registration is just $20 and is available online, at lbisfc.com/sign-up-weigh-in , or at one of the three participating tackle shops: Fisherman’s Headquarters in Ship Bottom, Jingles Bait and Tackle in Beach Haven and Surf City Bait and Tackle. These shops also serve as the weigh-in stations for the contest. For more information, including all tournament rules and requirements, visit lbisfc.com/lbi-spring-surf-fishing-tournament . Check out the three tackle shops’ Facebook pages as well for updates, or give them a call at 609-494-5739 (Fisherman’s Headquarters), 609-492-2795 (Jingles) or 609-494-2333 (Surf City).

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘A Grand Night For Singing’
events.description: A Grand Night For Singing Taste and imagination, the two key ingredients for a first-rate revue, abound in this fresh take on the Rodgers & Hammerstein canon. This new R&H musical opened the 1994 Broadway season with flair and distinction, garnering wildly enthusiastic notices and earning two Tony nominations, including Best Musical. With innovative musical arrangements, including “I’m Gonna Wash That Man Right Outa My Hair,” “Shall We Dance,” and “How Do You Solve A Problem Like Maria,” proving how terrifically up-to-date the remarkable songs of R&H remain. May 24, 28, 31, June 2, 4, 7 at 2:00 p.m. May 23, 24, 29, 30, June 4, 5, 6 at 8:00 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Barnegat Pride Holds Pride Festival
events.description: The festival will have a full day including stage performances with opening ceremonies followed by entertainment, musical acts and a DJ. Featured performers include Redbird Flying Solo and Alaska Neyenhouse. There will also be various vendors and food trucks. Desiree Moonshine returns as emcee. Suggested donation is $5. In efforts to give back to the community, we have partnered with Waretown United Methodist Church and are asking all attendees to please bring nonperishable, non-expired, kid-friendly food for their summer feeding program that serves children of Barnegat and Waretown. Barnegat Pride isn’t just for people in the LGBTQ community. We welcome family members, friends and people who are interested in supporting us. For more information, visit the Barnegat Pride Facebook page.

events.title: Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘A Grand Night For Singing’
events.description: A Grand Night For Singing Taste and imagination, the two key ingredients for a first-rate revue, abound in this fresh take on the Rodgers & Hammerstein canon. This new R&H musical opened the 1994 Broadway season with flair and distinction, garnering wildly enthusiastic notices and earning two Tony nominations, including Best Musical. With innovative musical arrangements, including “I’m Gonna Wash That Man Right Outa My Hair,” “Shall We Dance,” and “How Do You Solve A Problem Like Maria,” proving how terrifically up-to-date the remarkable songs of R&H remain. May 24, 28, 31, June 2, 4, 7 at 2:00 p.m. May 23, 24, 29, 30, June 4, 5, 6 at 8:00 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

location.address: 201 Engleside Ave, Beach Haven, New Jersey, 08008

events.title: Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.description: Dive into native plants of Ocean County and have some fun as you create your own "Make and Take" Container garden. events.title: Enjoy Capital Singers of Trenton Annual Shore Concert at Holy Innocents' Episcopal Church
events.description: All are welcome to join us for a wonderful afternoon of engaging music celebrating the 250th Anniversary of Our Nation. The program will include selections by Nathaniel Dett, Roland Carter, Randall Thompson and more! Capital Singers of Trenton (CST) is a non-sectarian, non-partisan, semi-professional choral organization founded in 2006. CST is committed to promoting the art of choral singing, enriching the cultural life of New Jersey, and serving as a goodwill ambassador for the city of Trenton. Under Artistic Director Vinroy D. Brown, Jr., the organization performs a repertoire of sacred and secular choral works reflecting various musical styles and historical periods. Free will offering ~ a light reception will follow the concert For more information, please call the church office 609-492-7571

events.description: Session Dates: May 5-28 and June 2-25 $165 per session Instructors Name: Mike and Maggie The LIT/Lifeguard Prep Class is designed to prepare youth and teens for future participation in the Lifeguard in Training program and eventual lifeguard certification. This is a preparatory, non-certifying program intended to build skills and confidence before formal LIT or lifeguard certification courses. Register at front desk or online at stfrancisparishlbi.org

events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

events.title: Split Decision
The Spring Derby is Here – Beloved Annual Surf Fishing Tournament Has Kicked Off
events.description: The Spring Derby is here – this beloved annual surf fishing tournament kicked off last weekend, and will run until 6 p.m. on June 21. For three months, anglers can target striped bass and bluefish from the beaches of Long Beach Island to win cash prizes. Fish must be caught by means of rod and reel in accordance with New Jersey state and federal regulations, and fish must be caught from the shores of LBI only – the ocean, inlet, bay and jetties. No stripers or blues caught by boat are accepted for this tournament. Registration is just $20 and is available online, at lbisfc.com/sign-up-weigh-in , or at one of the three participating tackle shops: Fisherman’s Headquarters in Ship Bottom, Jingles Bait and Tackle in Beach Haven and Surf City Bait and Tackle. These shops also serve as the weigh-in stations for the contest. For more information, including all tournament rules and requirements, visit lbisfc.com/lbi-spring-surf-fishing-tournament . Check out the three tackle shops’ Facebook pages as well for updates, or give them a call at 609-494-5739 (Fisherman’s Headquarters), 609-492-2795 (Jingles) or 609-494-2333 (Surf City).

events.title: Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Guys And Dolls’
events.description: Guys And Dolls Considered by many to be the perfect musical comedy, Guys and Dolls ran for 1,200 performances when it opened on Broadway in 1950. It received nearly unanimous positive reviews from critics and won a bevy of awards, including Tony Awards, Drama Desks and Oliviers. Set in a mythical New York City, Guys and Dolls is an oddball romantic comedy that takes us from the heart of Times Square to the cafes of Havana, Cuba, and even into the sewers of New York City, but eventually everyone ends up right where they belong. A treat for audiences and performers of any age. events.title: Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Beach Haven Library Offers Storytime
events.description: Join us for interactive stories and songs. Storytime sparks imagination and creativity in young children. Listening to books enhances the development of vocabulary, comprehension and language skills. These important communication skills are the building blocks of literacy and learning. Storytime events will take place weekly throughout the summer for toddlers through third grade. Each week will follow the same schedule for approximately 30 minutes. Programming will run for 11 weeks from June 16th through August 27th from 11-11:30 a.m. A parent and/or guardian must be present with the child for the duration of the program. Registration is now open. Registration is required for attendance. Register at the circulation desk ot call 609-492-7081. No bare feet. Children must wear shoes or sandals to follow health and safety guidelines. Please register each child individually. Limited to 24 child participants per program/date. events.title: Lifeguard in Training (LIT) Prep Clinic
events.description: Session Dates: May 5-28 and June 2-25 $165 per session Instructors Name: Mike and Maggie The LIT/Lifeguard Prep Class is designed to prepare youth and teens for future participation in the Lifeguard in Training program and eventual lifeguard certification. This class focuses on developing swim endurance, physical readiness, water confidence, and foundational rescue knowledge in a structured, supportive environment. This is a preparatory, non-certifying program intended to build skills and confidence before formal LIT or lifeguard certification courses. Register at front desk or online at stfrancisparishlbi.org

events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

events.title: Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: Gab Cinque  Band
Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Irish Tenor Emmet Cahill of Celtic Thunder Performs at St. Francis of Assisi Parish
events.description: Join us for a wonderful afternoon of music with world acclaimed Irish Tenor Emmet Cahill of Celtic Thunder on Sunday, June 28 at St. Francis of Assisi Parish, Long Beach Island, NJ. Fresh off his sold-out Carnegie Hall debut, a national tour in Australia, and a 55-city U.S. tour with Celtic Thunder, Emmet is back on tour across North America. He recently also performed on national television with the world-famous Tabernacle Choir. Enjoy an unforgettable performance featuring classic Irish songs, Broadway favorites, beloved church hymns, and more, including “Danny Boy,” “Toora Loora,” “Amazing Grace,” and “How Great Thou Art.” This is an afternoon you will not want to miss. Sunday, June 28 3 pm Doors open at 2:15 pm $40 General Admission Tickets Available at events.ticketleap.com

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Beginnings - Chicago Tribute Band’
events.description: The Ultimate Chicago Tribute Band! Beginnings formed in 2002 as a tribute to the music of super-group Chicago. The band is based in New York City and regularly performs for highly enthusiastic and loyal audiences all over the United States and beyond. Performing in Chicago’s original, classic-era 7pc. Configuration, Beginnings delivers the world’s most authentic live Chicago concert experience, bringing the legendary band’s extensive catalog to life, with stunning detail, accuracy, and intensity. Fueled by world class musicianship and a passion for performance, the band is a live music experience not to be missed. The group’s love of the music and dedication to their fans makes for an outstanding and electrifying show every time. Come see the band live, and they promise to “Make You Smile.” Note Two Show Times: June 29 at 5 pm and 8 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Guys And Dolls’
events.description: Guys And Dolls Considered by many to be the perfect musical comedy, Guys and Dolls ran for 1,200 performances when it opened on Broadway in 1950. It received nearly unanimous positive reviews from critics and won a bevy of awards, including Tony Awards, Drama Desks and Oliviers. Set in a mythical New York City, Guys and Dolls is an oddball romantic comedy that takes us from the heart of Times Square to the cafes of Havana, Cuba, and even into the sewers of New York City, but eventually everyone ends up right where they belong. A treat for audiences and performers of any age. events.title: Beach Haven Library Offers Storytime
events.description: Join us for interactive stories and songs. Storytime sparks imagination and creativity in young children. Listening to books enhances the development of vocabulary, comprehension and language skills. These important communication skills are the building blocks of literacy and learning. Storytime events will take place weekly throughout the summer for toddlers through third grade. Each week will follow the same schedule for approximately 30 minutes. Programming will run for 11 weeks from June 16th through August 27th from 11-11:30 a.m. A parent and/or guardian must be present with the child for the duration of the program. Registration is now open. Registration is required for attendance. Register at the circulation desk ot call 609-492-7081. No bare feet. Children must wear shoes or sandals to follow health and safety guidelines. Please register each child individually. Limited to 24 child participants per program/date. Age Groups Toddler (18-36 months) Tuesdays Preschool (3-5 years) Wednesdays Elementary (6-8 years) Thursdays

events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

events.title: Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: 3AM Tokyo Fireworks Party
Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Young Frankenstein’
events.description: Young Frankenstein The comedy genius, Mel Brooks, adapts his legendarily funny film into a brilliant stage creation – Young Frankenstein! Grandson of the infamous Victor Frankenstein, Frederick Frankenstein (pronounced “Fronk-en-steen”) inherits his family’s estate in Transylvania. With the help of a hunchbacked sidekick, Igor (pronounced “Eye-gore”), and a leggy lab assistant, Inga (pronounced normally), Frederick finds himself in the mad scientist shoes of his ancestors. “It’s alive!” he exclaims as he brings to life a creature to rival his grandfathers. Eventually, of course, the monster escapes and hilarity continuously abounds. Young Frankenstein is scientifically proven, monstrously good entertainment. July 9, 12, 14, 16, 19, 21, 26 at 2:00 p.m. July 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, 18, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25 at 8:00 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

Grandson of the infamous Victor Frankenstein, Frederick Frankenstein (pronounced “Fronk-en-steen”) inherits his family’s estate in Transylvania. With the help of a hunchbacked sidekick, Igor (pronounced “Eye-gore”), and a leggy lab assistant, Inga (pronounced normally), Frederick finds himself in the mad scientist shoes of his ancestors. “It’s alive!” he exclaims as he brings to life a creature to rival his grandfathers. Eventually, of course, the monster escapes and hilarity continuously abounds. Young Frankenstein is scientifically proven, monstrously good entertainment. July 9, 12, 14, 16, 19, 21, 26 at 2:00 p.m. July 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, 18, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25 at 8:00 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Split Decision
Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: Gab Cinque  Band
Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Just Sixties Performs at Surflight Theatre
events.description: Since their formation in 1982, JUST SIXTIES has played at top clubs and has been the opening act for Paul Revere and the Raiders, Felix Cavaliere’s Rascals and Jay and the Americans. Their repertoire includes the ultimate cross-section of Sixties music from “Yummy, Yummy, Yummy” to Jimi Hendrix’ “Fire,” including many one-hit wonders. You’ll hear authentic renditions of the timeless classics from The Beatles, The Beach Boys, The Doors, The Rolling Stones, Janis Joplin, The Monkees, and so much more. Note Two Show Times: July 13 at 5 pm and 8 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Beach Haven Library Offers Storytime
events.description: Join us for interactive stories and songs. Storytime sparks imagination and creativity in young children. Listening to books enhances the development of vocabulary, comprehension and language skills. These important communication skills are the building blocks of literacy and learning. Storytime events will take place weekly throughout the summer for toddlers through third grade. Each week will follow the same schedule for approximately 30 minutes. Programming will run for 11 weeks from June 16th through August 27th from 11-11:30 a.m. A parent and/or guardian must be present with the child for the duration of the program. Registration is now open. Registration is required for attendance. Register at the circulation desk ot call 609-492-7081. No bare feet. Children must wear shoes or sandals to follow health and safety guidelines. Please register each child individually. Limited to 24 child participants per program/date. Age Groups Toddler (18-36 months) Tuesdays Preschool (3-5 years) Wednesdays Elementary (6-8 years) Thursdays

events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

events.title: The Way Outs
Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Beach Haven Library Offers Storytime
events.description: Join us for interactive stories and songs. Storytime sparks imagination and creativity in young children. Listening to books enhances the development of vocabulary, comprehension and language skills. These important communication skills are the building blocks of literacy and learning. Storytime events will take place weekly throughout the summer for toddlers through third grade. Each week will follow the same schedule for approximately 30 minutes. Programming will run for 11 weeks from June 16th through August 27th from 11-11:30 a.m. A parent and/or guardian must be present with the child for the duration of the program. Registration is now open. Registration is required for attendance. Register at the circulation desk ot call 609-492-7081. No bare feet. Children must wear shoes or sandals to follow health and safety guidelines. Please register each child individually. Limited to 24 child participants per program/date. events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

events.title: The Cliffs
Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: 3AM Tokyo
Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Back To The Garden 1969 - Woodstock Tribute Show at Surflight Theatre
events.description: Formed by Gary Adamson in 2009, this is a group of versatile musicians and singers who have come together to celebrate the music, and spirit, of the Woodstock Era. The band recreated the classic sounds associated with Woodstock. The musical performances are a tribute to the artists who made Woodstock great, including Crosby, Stills, Nash & Young, Jimi Hendrix, Sly & The Family Stone, Jefferson Airplane, Santana, Joe Cocker, Janis Joplin, The Band, Creedence Clearwater Revival, Canned Heat, Ten Years After, The Who, Mountain, Richie Havens, Melanie, Arlo Guthrie, Blood, Sweat & Tears, The Grateful Dead, Country Joe & The Fish,… and more! BTTG 1969 brings you a full multimedia theatrical event, recreating the Woodstock Experience through period costuming, lighting, video, and sound bites. Storytelling also enhances the mood, with tales of the festival, the legendary artists who performed, and the backdrop of the tumultuous decade of the 60’s. Most tribute bands perform the music of only one artist. BTTG 1969 is a tribute to a very special moment in time, and to the many artists whose music will forever be known as “Classic Rock.” From nostalgic Baby Boomers to intrigued Gen Z’ers, and all those in between, there is something in this show for everyone! So grab your tie dye and love beads … and celebrate Woodstock. Note Two Show Times: August 3 at 5 pm and 8 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

events.title: Kono Nation
Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: Aftershock
Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Taylor Mason - Family Comedy Night’
events.description: Taylor Mason brings his one-of-a-kind persona to Surflight. Taylor is a ventriloquist, musician, comedy magician and stand-up comedian who blends observational humor, keen musicianship and state-of-the-art ventriloquism. Taylor Mason will present a positive, hilarious, unforgettable program that connects people thru those things that bond us: laughter, music, magic and the shared experience of something special, something original, and something unforgettable. The audience will not be mere spectators; rather they will participate in an interactive experience that will entertain all, from young children to parents and grandparents. August 10 at 6 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

Lawton. Once upon a time in the late '80s, unlikely soulmates Vivian and Edward overcame all odds to find each other…and themselves. events.title: Antique Car and Truck Show at Barnegat American Legion
events.description: The John Wesley Taylor American Legion Post 232 will be hosting an Antique Car and Truck Show. The post is partnering with Sentimental Cruisers Car Club, in memory of their past President and Air Force Veteran, Bill Moglia. They are one of the premier Antique Car Clubs in Ocean County. This event is free to the public. Music by Tommy Tunes along with food and drinks available. Bring the family for an exciting day of seeing vehicles of all ages. Come out and support this great cause in dedication to Bill’s memory. If anyone would like to enter their vehicle in the show, please complete the entry form at the Post. Applications are inside the canteen. You may also enter through our facebook account at www.facebook.com/alabarnegat or www.barnegatamericanlegion.com . The pre-registration fee is $15. Date of show registration is $20. For more information, please call the post 609-698-9876.

events.title: Beach Haven Library Offers Storytime
events.description: Join us for interactive stories and songs. Storytime sparks imagination and creativity in young children. Listening to books enhances the development of vocabulary, comprehension and language skills. These important communication skills are the building blocks of literacy and learning. Storytime events will take place weekly throughout the summer for toddlers through third grade. Each week will follow the same schedule for approximately 30 minutes. Programming will run for 11 weeks from June 16th through August 27th from 11-11:30 a.m. A parent and/or guardian must be present with the child for the duration of the program. Registration is now open. Registration is required for attendance. Register at the circulation desk ot call 609-492-7081. No bare feet. Children must wear shoes or sandals to follow health and safety guidelines. Please register each child individually. Limited to 24 child participants per program/date. events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

events.title: Winslow Eagles Tribute Band Performs at Surflight Theatre
events.description: East Coast’s Greatest Eagles Tribute Band. Winslow Tribute Band celebrates the iconic American Band The Eagles. This powerhouse ensemble recreates the live performance of The Eagles and the signature sound that made them one of the most successful artists in the 70’s. Winslow’s band members are seasoned musicians who have a deep love for the Eagles’ music, and strive to recreate the magic of their live performances. From ‘Hotel California’ to ‘Take It Easy,’ they bring the Eagles’ greatest hits to life on stage. With a passion for detail, Winslow’s multi-lead vocalists authentically capture the soaring 5-part harmonies, precise instrumentation and stage showmanship as they perform The Eagles music catalog of greatest hits, deep cuts, as well as some of the solo work of Don Henley and Joe Walsh. From their outstanding emulation of the a cappella harmonies of “Seven Bridges Road” to the precision of dueling guitars in “Hotel California,” Winslow deliver a true “Eagles” concert experience you will not forget. August 24 at 5 pm and 8 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Barnegat Township Jersey Fresh Farm Market Announce New Location
events.description: The Barnegat Township Recreation Department sponsors Jersey Fresh farm markets on Thursdays, beginning June 11 from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. at a new location – in the municipal building parking at 900 West Bay Ave. It will run through Sept. 17. Admission is free. Participating farms include Krowicki Farms, Stilwell Farms and Eckert Farms. Children’s play area and air-conditioned restrooms. Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

location.address: 900 W Bay Ave, Barnegat Township, New Jersey, 08005

events.title: Beach Haven Library Offers Storytime
events.description: Join us for interactive stories and songs. Storytime sparks imagination and creativity in young children. Listening to books enhances the development of vocabulary, comprehension and language skills. These important communication skills are the building blocks of literacy and learning. Storytime events will take place weekly throughout the summer for toddlers through third grade. Each week will follow the same schedule for approximately 30 minutes. Programming will run for 11 weeks from June 16th through August 27th from 11-11:30 a.m. A parent and/or guardian must be present with the child for the duration of the program. Registration is now open. Registration is required for attendance. Register at the circulation desk ot call 609-492-7081. No bare feet. Children must wear shoes or sandals to follow health and safety guidelines. Please register each child individually. Limited to 24 child participants per program/date. Age Groups Toddler (18-36 months) Tuesdays Preschool (3-5 years) Wednesdays Elementary (6-8 years) Thursdays

events.title: The Way Outs
Saturday Night Concert Series at Barnegat Dock
events.description: Joey D and Johnny B’s Rockin’ Oldies band will open the Barnegat Township Recreation Department’s free Saturday night concert series at the dock on June 6. All concerts begin at 6:30. Well known in the area, the band performs a wide range of music featuring doo-wop, Motown and disco among others. They also have return engagements on July 4 and Aug. 29. The rest of the schedule includes Sky’s The Limit, June 13, July 11 and Aug. 1; Jukebox Legends, June 27 and Aug. 8; the Fireflies, June 20 and Aug. 22; The Kootz Band, July 18; Brass Tacks, July 25; RB Express, Aug. 15. There is also a special Friday, July 3 concert featuring Remember When. Ice cream, snacks and bottled water will be available for sale. In case of rain, concerts will be moved to the Russell Brackman Middle School auditorium. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122, or visit barnegat.net .

events.title: Sky City Social
Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Ghost The Musical’
events.description: Ghost The Musical Based on the iconic 1990 film Ghost this romantic fantasy is brought to the stage, blending heartfelt emotion, gripping suspense, and supernatural wonder. The story follows Sam Wheat, a successful banker, and Molly Jensen, an artist, who are deeply in love. Their lives are shattered when Sam is killed during a mugging. However, Sam’s spirit remains on Earth, discovering that his death was not an accident but part of a larger conspiracy. Molly struggles to process her grief, while Sam realizes the power of love and his unfinished business on Earth. Ghost The Musical captivates audiences with its blend of romance, humor, and enduring love. September 3, 6, 8, 10, 13, 15, 20 at 2:00 p.m. September 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 at 8:00 p.m. September 8, 9, 10, 12, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19 at 7:30 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

Approximately 40 vendors have signed up for the summer selling a wide variety of merchandise, including baked goods, ground coffee, wines and spirits, smoothies, sauces, pastas, handbags and accessories, pet products, micro green plants, home decor, cleansing oil, crab cakes, pickles and more. For more information, call the recreation department at 609-698-0080, extension 122 or visit barnegat.net and click on “office of recreation.”

location.address: 900 W Bay Ave, Barnegat Township, New Jersey, 08005

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Ghost The Musical’
events.description: Ghost The Musical Based on the iconic 1990 film Ghost this romantic fantasy is brought to the stage, blending heartfelt emotion, gripping suspense, and supernatural wonder. The story follows Sam Wheat, a successful banker, and Molly Jensen, an artist, who are deeply in love. Their lives are shattered when Sam is killed during a mugging. However, Sam’s spirit remains on Earth, discovering that his death was not an accident but part of a larger conspiracy. Molly struggles to process her grief, while Sam realizes the power of love and his unfinished business on Earth. Ghost The Musical captivates audiences with its blend of romance, humor, and enduring love. September 3, 6, 8, 10, 13, 15, 20 at 2:00 p.m. September 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 at 8:00 p.m. September 8, 9, 10, 12, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19 at 7:30 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: An Evening with Groucho at Surflight Theatre
events.description: An Evening with Groucho Dubbed “nothing short of masterful” by the Chicago Tribune, award-winning actor Frank Ferrante recreates his PBS, New York and London acclaimed stage portrayal of legendary comedian in this fast paced 90 minutes of hilarity. The two-act comedy consists of the best Groucho one-liners, anecdotes and songs including “Hooray for Captain Spalding,” and “Lydia, the Tattooed Lady.” The audience literally becomes part of the show as Ferrante ad-libs his way throughout the performance in grand Groucho style. Accompanied by his onstage pianist, Ferrante portrays the young Groucho of stage and film and reacquaints us with the likes of brothers Harpo, Chico, Zeppo and Gummo, Charlie Chaplin, W.C. Fields and Marx foil Margaret Dumont. A show perfect for all ages! September 11 at 6 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Vanities: The Musical’
events.description: Vanities: The Musical Vanities: The Musical chronicles the life-affirming journey of three vivacious Texas teens from cheerleaders to sorority sisters to housewives to liberated women and beyond. This musical captures a snapshot-sharp portrait of the lives, loves, disappointments, and dreams of these young women growing up during the turbulent sixties and seventies and reconnecting in the late 1980s. With a tunefully evocative score by David Kirshenbaum (Summer of ’42) and Jack Heifner’s hilarious adaptation of his long-running Off-Broadway smash, Vanities: The Musical is a funny and poignant look at three best friends who discover that, through thirty years of rapidly changing times, the one thing they can rely on is each other. September 23, 24, 27, 29, 30, October 1, 4 at 2:00 p.m. September 23, 24, 26, October 1, 2, 3 at 7:30 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Deceived’
events.description: Deceived Deceived, based on the original play Gaslight, is a psychological thriller that will leave audiences on the edge of their seat. A suspense-filled storyline, Deceived delves into the depths of manipulation, deception, and paranoia as it delivers a chilling outcome that you won’t soon forget. Bella Manningham thinks she’s losing her mind. In the evenings, she hears strange sounds and the gaslights dim for no apparent reason. But is she losing her grip on reason, or is it being loosened for her? Will the gaslight have its intended, murderous, effect? location.address: 201 Engleside Ave, Beach Haven, New Jersey, 08008

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Deceived’
events.description: Deceived Deceived, based on the original play Gaslight, is a psychological thriller that will leave audiences on the edge of their seat. A suspense-filled storyline, Deceived delves into the depths of manipulation, deception, and paranoia as it delivers a chilling outcome that you won’t soon forget. Bella Manningham thinks she’s losing her mind. In the evenings, she hears strange sounds and the gaslights dim for no apparent reason. But is she losing her grip on reason, or is it being loosened for her? Will the gaslight have its intended, murderous, effect? October 7, 8, 11 at 2:00 p.m. October 7, 8, 9, 10 at 7:30 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Barnegat Quakers Hold Weekly Worship
events.description: Weekly Worship For an hour every Sunday at 10 am, the Barnegat Quakers gather in the living silence, as others have done on our Meeting’s benches for hundreds of years. In that quiet space we feel the peace that refreshes and renews us. As moved, we share our thoughts - our vocal ministry. After worship we enjoy each other’s company, and those who are available continue the fellowship over a breakfast out together. Newcomers are our guests. Monthly - Friends, Food and Fellowship The Barnegat Friends host an open house brunch the fourth Sunday every month. We worship at 10 am, then enjoy fellowship from 11 am until the last person leaves.

events.title: La Traviata by Center Stage Opera Co. at Surflight Theatre
events.description: Presented by Center Stage Opera. Violetta Valéry, a young courtesan, famous in the Parisian high society, throws a party in her home. Here, Gaston, Viscount of Letorières, introduces a new admirer to her, Alfredo Germont. He confesses that the moment he saw her, he was fascinated by her beauty and intelligence, though he has never had the courage to tell her. He toasts in her honor before the whole crowd. November 14 at 7:30 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Enjoy Holidays with The Duprees at Surflight Theatre
events.description: Holidays with The Duprees Celebrate the season with The Duprees—Tommy Petillo, Jimmy Spinelli, and Shawn Stevens—as they return to Surflight Theatre for a heartwarming evening of holiday cheer and timeless classics. Known for their smooth harmonies and golden-era charm, The Duprees will perform a joyful mix of seasonal favorites and romantic standards, including “Jingle Bell Rock,” “Sleigh Ride,” “You Belong to Me,” and “Why Don’t You Believe Me?.” Whether you're starting a new tradition or reliving cherished memories, this concert promises warmth, joy, and musical magic to kick off the holiday season. November 22 at 2 pm For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Holiday Inn’
events.description: Holiday Inn Irving Berlin’s Holiday Inn tells the story of Jim, who leaves the bright lights of show business behind to settle down on his farmhouse in Connecticut ... but life just isn’t the same without a bit of song and dance. Jim’s luck takes a spectacular turn when he meets Linda, a spirited schoolteacher with talent to spare. Together they turn the farmhouse into a fabulous inn with dazzling performances to celebrate each holiday, from Thanksgiving to the Fourth of July. Based on the classic film, this joyous musical features thrilling dance numbers, laugh-out-loud comedy and a parade of hit Irving Berlin songs, including “Blue Skies,” “Easter Parade,” “Steppin’ Out With My Baby,” “Heat Wave,” “White Christmas” and many more! November 28, 29, December 2, 3, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 13, 16, 17, 19, 20 at 2:00 p.m. November 28, December 4, 5, 11, 12, 18, 19 at 7:30 p.m. All shows, dates, times. are subject to change For tickets visit surflight.org

events.title: Surflight Theatre Presents ‘Holiday Inn’
events.description: Holiday Inn Irving Berlin’s Holiday Inn tells the story of Jim, who leaves the bright lights of show business behind to settle down on his farmhouse in Connecticut ... but life just isn’t the same without a bit of song and dance. Jim’s luck takes a spectacular turn when he meets Linda, a spirited schoolteacher with talent to spare. Together they turn the farmhouse into a fabulous inn with dazzling performances to celebrate each holiday, from Thanksgiving to the Fourth of July. Based on the classic film, this joyous musical features thrilling dance numbers, laugh-out-loud comedy and a parade of hit Irving Berlin songs, including “Blue Skies,” “Easter Parade,” “Steppin’ Out With My Baby,” “Heat Wave,” “White Christmas” and many more! events.title: New Year’s Eve Bubbly Bash with The Rave-Ons at Surflight Theatre
events.description: New Year’s Eve Bubbly Bash with The Rave-Ons The Rave-Ons return to the Surflight Theatre on New Year’s Eve! Todd Meredith & The Rave-Ons are a high energy rock and roll band originally formed to revitalize the music of Buddy Holly and the Crickets. They produce and star in two different Buddy Holly tribute shows as well as a 50’s and 60’s rock and roll tribute. For concerts, they have over a hundred and fifty rock and roll tunes from all decades in their repertoire. They have played together all over the world, dazzling audiences at such venues as: Celebrity Cruise Lines, Holland America Cruise Lines, and BB Kings Blues Club, NYC and numerous other clubs, theaters, restaurants, and civic centers from coast to coast – including Buddy Holly’s hometown of Lubbock, Texas! Audiences agree, the Rave-Ons give their shows an energy and exuberance that cannot be matched! Note Two Show Times: December 31 at 5 pm and 8 pm For tickets visit surflight.org
